video_title: Tyler Perry’s Beauty in Black: Season 2 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
This movie was absolutely DISGUSTING. Beauty in Black is one of the lowest things Tyler Perry has ever put out. Every single chance he gets, he’s forcing the same tired narrative of homosexuality into the story, as if Black audiences can’t get through one of his films without being bombarded with it. It’s not art, it’s not empowering, it’s agenda-pushing — plain and simple.
And don’t even get me started on the character Kimmie. Tyler Perry went out of his way to make her look like the worst kind of stereotype — low, dumb, and absolutely degrading as a Black woman. Instead of showing strength, class, or any depth, she’s written to be a joke. It’s insulting. Perry has made a career out of tearing down Black women on screen, and Kimmie is just the latest example of that mockery.
Honestly, I walked away from this film feeling sick. There’s nothing “beautiful” about Beauty in Black. It’s messy, disrespectful, and a slap in the face to Black audiences who keep giving Tyler Perry the benefit of the doubt. At this point, he’s not even trying to uplift us — he’s exploiting us for views while recycling the same tired, toxic portrayals over and over.
Tyler Perry should be ashamed. This isn’t representation, it’s humiliation.

video_title: Tyler Perry’s Beauty in Black: Season 2 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I see some repressed chemistry between Roy and Kimmy. The communication between them is direct and genuine; although hurtful and heavy. She's truely beautiful unlike Mallori. She's authentic and wild. What destroys Roy is the fakeness and lack of communication skills, llistening skills and empathetic skills of Mallori. He tried to reach to her at times. But Mallori is just a cold b. He could have expressed his problems to Kimmy in the beginning but he has zero respect for people working in clubs. He didn't know that she would have the intelligence to help him.

 Kimmy freely expresses her hurt to Roy. She doesn't play pretend about Roy. Mallori acts as a fake in public and is unnecessarily mean and sarcastic towards Roy. Seems like Kimmy has something special that can help Roy change. And Roy can help her toughen up.

On one hand, Mallori seemed way too strong to Roy. On the end of the spectrum,  Kim was a weak duckling from the club.  Survival of the fittest.Therefore, Roy saw Kim as an easy target,a punching bag. Roy despises both women because one is too masculine and the other one has the title of a prostitute.

 
Kimmy was forced to work in a club and becomes addicted with revenge. Roy on the other hand is addicted with cocaine, confrontations, slut shaming and put downs. When Mallori puts him down, he takes out his anger on other women.

Both Kimmy and Roy have had a difficult childhood. They weren't loved by their parents. They are constantly hurt by their close ones. Roy is neither loved by his parents nor by his wife.

Might be a fake camaraderie but I see something quite friendly going on between Mallori and the lawyer. Mallori is disrespectful towards most people in the show except towards the lawyer. She's friendly with him just to manipulate him but it's still endearing to see how her character softens when she is around him..

video_title: Tyler Perry’s Beauty in Black: Season 2 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Honestly, the dialogues are too long sometimes with so little substance in it at the same time. The drunk Olivia scene was too long for nothing, just for her to discover the affair between her son and varney. The fact that she was hitting on varney brought nothing to the intrigue. For some dialogues I just skip or make the video faster because it's always the same scenario, two characters meet, they exchange tense greetings, i can skip for 2 min and still not miss where the intrigue is going. It's ridiculous. And the lack of vocabulary, all those curse words, it really makes this show cheap, it honestly feels like a pre teen is writing it, not even a teen. F words, N words, S words, you can't go through a scene without it, it is sincerely preposterous. And the characters in every scene are always trying to prove themselves as being tough, muscles shown every scene "you know who I am?! You don't know how I am! Imma show you! Nobody messes up with me", put those words in every main character (male or female), in every exchange they have, and you will see that it fits. Only the developing mature and respectful relationship between kimmie and horace softens up this vulgar show. I won't be watching season 3, the scenarios have all run out, we can see where it's going, and judging from season 2, no real growth is to be excpected from the characters (not necessarily in a good way for everyone, but also in a deeper negative way for some), because if they keep wanting to make vulgar words at every sentence their trademark, there is very little to be expected.

comment_text:
Here's a true story. About 30 years ago, the City of Milano (Italy) built these brand new BEAUTIFUL apartment buildings. Three of them around a courtyard. There were so many Italian families that needed housing. As soon as the buildings were finished, the Roma started showing up. One by one, they occupied EVERY apartment. There were at least 200 apartments. Italian law refuses to throw occupying people out of an apartment if there's a baby or a pregnant woman, so these Roma had all their relatives from all over Europe come and move into the apartments with their babies and pregnant family members and others.
   It took YEARS before the city government built new apartments after that!!
  Btw, the babies they take with them to panhandle have been shown to be drugged so they'll stay asleep. Europe is overflowing with these crooks. I live in Italy (am American) and the place is chock-full of them!!
